# VERIFY: #73 â€” fix(start-feature-planning): move branch creation before planning to prevent stall on spec work
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output -->

**Intent:** Verify that the implementation satisfies the spec intent â€” branch creation in `start-feature-planning` precedes all planning work, and working tree is clean after every play completes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After `start-feature-planning` completes, all STM artifacts (`spec.md`, `verify.md`, `tasks.md`, checkpoint, evidence) reside on the feature branch, not on main
- [ ] No STM artifacts are written to `main` at any point during `start-feature-planning` execution
- [ ] After `commit-code` completes, `git status` shows a clean working tree (no untracked or modified files)
- [ ] After `create-pr` completes, `git status` shows a clean working tree
- [ ] After `ship` completes, `git status` shows a clean working tree on main
- [ ] The evidence-commit logic exists in exactly one file (`commit-stm-evidence/SKILL.md`) and is not duplicated in any play SKILL.md
- [ ] `repo-orchestrator.md` lists `commit-stm-evidence` as an available skill with intent mapping
- [ ] If user Vanishes at the `start-feature-planning` approval gate, the feature branch is deleted from remote and working tree is clean
- [ ] Subsequent invocation of any play after another play completes passes C1 pre-flight (clean tree)
- [ ] atomic play agent limits are not violated (commit-code and create-pr still use â‰¤2 distinct agents)

## Verification Steps

| Step | Method | Expected Outcome |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| V1 | Trace phase execution order in updated `start-feature-planning/SKILL.md` | Branch creation step (Step 1b) appears before Step 2 (Plan sub-agent) and Step 3 (artifact writes) |
| V2 | Read Write calls for planning artifacts in `start-feature-planning/SKILL.md` | All artifact paths target `.meridian/{issue}/planning/` and branch exists before any writes |
| V3 | Grep for `chore(stm)` or `commit.*evidence` across all play SKILL.md files | Only `commit-stm-evidence/SKILL.md` contains the implementation; plays contain only a delegation call to `repo-orchestrator` |
| V4 | Read Report step of `commit-code/SKILL.md` | After writing evidence file, calls `repo-orchestrator` with intent to commit STM files; non-blocking |
| V5 | Read Report step of `create-pr/SKILL.md` | After writing evidence file, calls `repo-orchestrator` with intent to commit STM files; non-blocking |
| V6 | Read Report step of `ship/SKILL.md` | After writing evidence file, calls `repo-orchestrator` with intent to commit STM files; post-merge context documented |
| V7 | Read `repo-orchestrator.md` skills table | `commit-stm-evidence` appears in Available Skills and Intent Recognition sections |
| V8 | Read `commit-stm-evidence/SKILL.md` | Accepts `files` list and `issue_number`; produces a `chore(stm):` conventional commit; returns `committed`, `hash`, `files_committed` |
| V9 | Read Tether/Vanish handling in updated `start-feature-planning/SKILL.md` | On Vanish after branch exists, play invokes `repo-orchestrator` to delete branch before halting |
| V10 | Count distinct agent names in each play's phase table | `commit-code`: 2 (repo-orchestrator, project-orchestrator). `create-pr`: 1 (repo-orchestrator). `ship`: 2 (repo-orchestrator + L1s via Skill tool) |

## Edge Cases

| Scenario | Expected Behavior |
|----------|------------------|
| `start-feature-planning` interrupted after branch creation but before planning artifacts written | Branch exists on remote; artifacts missing; no resume mode (out of scope) â€” user can check out branch manually |
| `start-feature-planning` Vanish after branch exists | Play calls `repo-orchestrator` to delete the remote branch; working tree returns to clean state on `main` |
| `commit-stm-evidence` called with a non-existent file | Skill verifies file existence before staging; skips missing files with warning; returns `committed: false` if nothing to stage; play logs warning, does not halt |
| `ship` calls `commit-code` which writes and commits its own evidence, then `ship` writes its own evidence | `ship` evidence written after `commit-code` completes; `ship` Report step calls `commit-stm-evidence` for its own evidence; both committed independently |
| Evidence commit creates an extra commit in the PR | Expected and desirable â€” `chore(stm):` prefix clearly marks it |
| `create-pr` evidence committed after PR exists | Correct â€” evidence captures the PR result; committed after creation succeeds |
| `commit-stm-evidence` returns `committed: false` (e.g. files already staged by something else) | Play logs warning, does not halt â€” evidence commit is always non-blocking |
